title: Python Data and Functions
subtitle: Organize data, write reusable code, and process files
intendedFor: Learners who know basic Python syntax and want to work with collections, functions, files, exceptions, and simple data-cleaning tasks.

courseOverview.recommendedSequence: Complete modules in order. This course builds from collections, to functions, to file-based data processing.
courseOverview.summary: This course teaches learners how to organize multiple values, write reusable functions, read and write files, handle exceptions, and clean simple data.

moduleSummary.title: Lists, Tuples, and Dictionaries
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners how to organize multiple values and choose the right collection type.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a small contact book or inventory tracker using lists and dictionaries.

moduleSummary.title: Functions and Modularity
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Help learners organize code into reusable functions with clear inputs and outputs.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Refactor a calculator or text utility into several reusable functions.

moduleSummary.title: Files, Exceptions, and Data Cleaning
moduleSummary.coreSkill: Teach learners how to read/write files and handle messy data without crashing programs.
moduleSummary.outputFocus: Build a small log or CSV cleaner that reads a file, validates rows, and writes a summary.

authoringGuidance: This course must always use profileId python and subjectSlug python-data-functions-v2.
Do not use SQL datasets, SQL tables, sql_query recipes, datasetId, fixedSqlDialect, resultShape, or checkSql in Python lessons.
Python code_input exercises should prefer recipeType fixed_tests and include explicit stdin/stdout tests.
Write code_input solutions as complete runnable programs or function-based exercises with deterministic tests.
Keep examples focused on lists, dictionaries, functions, files, exceptions, and simple data cleaning.

assessmentAndDelivery.suggestedBeginnerRhythm: Two or three short lessons per week, with one module project after each module.
toolingSuggestions: Browser runner or local VS Code
Simple test runner for function-based exercises
assessmentAndDelivery.closingNote: Learners finish able to organize data, write reusable functions, and build small file-processing utilities.

modules.title: Lists, Tuples, and Dictionaries
modules.purpose: Teach learners how to organize multiple values and choose the right collection type.
learningObjectives: Use tuples for fixed grouped values
modules.moduleProject: Build a small contact book or inventory tracker using lists and dictionaries.

topics.title: Creating and Indexing Lists
topics.summary: Create lists and access items by position.

topics.title: List Methods and Mutation
topics.summary: Use append, remove, pop, sort, and understand mutation.

topics.title: Looping Through Lists
topics.summary: Process each list item and build derived results.

topics.title: Dictionary Basics
topics.summary: Store and retrieve values using keys.

topics.title: Updating and Looping Dictionaries
topics.summary: Add, update, remove, and loop through dictionary data.

topics.title: Nested Data Structures
topics.summary: Represent real-world data with lists of dictionaries and nested containers.

modules.title: Functions and Modularity
modules.purpose: Help learners organize code into reusable functions with clear inputs and outputs.
learningObjectives: Separate program logic into small pieces
modules.moduleProject: Refactor a calculator or text utility into several reusable functions.

topics.title: Defining and Calling Functions
topics.summary: Create functions and call them with arguments.

topics.title: Parameters and Return Values
topics.summary: Pass data into functions and return results.

topics.title: Print vs Return
topics.summary: Understand when to display output and when to return data.

topics.title: Scope and Local Variables
topics.summary: Understand where variables exist and why local scope matters.

topics.title: Decomposition and Refactoring
topics.summary: Break a larger task into clear helper functions.

topics.title: Docstrings and Function Contracts
topics.summary: Document what a function expects and returns.

modules.title: Files, Exceptions, and Data Cleaning
modules.purpose: Teach learners how to read/write files and handle messy data without crashing programs.
modules.moduleProject: Build a small log or CSV cleaner that reads a file, validates rows, and writes a summary.

topics.title: Reading Text Files
topics.summary: Open files, read text, and close resources safely.

topics.title: Writing Text Files
topics.summary: Write output to files using safe file modes.

topics.title: Working With Paths
topics.summary: Understand relative paths and simple file organization.

topics.title: Try/Except Basics
topics.summary: Catch expected errors and keep programs usable.

topics.title: Validating and Cleaning Input
topics.summary: Clean text, check missing values, and reject invalid rows.

topics.title: Simple CSV Processing
topics.summary: Read comma-separated rows and transform them into dictionaries.
